destinations.evidence: The duplicate-origin path compares canonical block/address/input/deadline context and replaces the entry when that context differs.
destinations.snippet:
self.precompile_address == other.precompile_address &&
self.precompile_input == other.precompile_input &&
self.commit_block == other.commit_block &&
self.max_poll_block == other.max_poll_block
...
if previous.has_same_canonical_context(&metadata) { return Ok(()); }
...
inner.transactions.insert(tx_hash, metadata)

destinations.evidence: Unchanged duplicates preserve current lifecycle state, while changed canonical context resets the entry and clears result/proof fields tied to the old context.
destinations.snippet:
execution_result: None,
execution_proof: None,
delivery_result: None,
delivery_proof: None,
zk_proof: None
...
duplicate_same_context_preserves_fulfilled_state
...
duplicate_changed_context_resets_to_awaiting_execution

equivalence_argument: The invariant is implemented by the same canonical-context refresh path as index 153. For the same original transaction hash, changed commit_block, precompile address/input, commitment transaction, or phase-2 deadline prevents the idempotent duplicate return and causes replacement with fresh AwaitingExecution metadata with all old result/proof fields cleared.
rationale: The duplicate-origin path now computes context_changed, refreshes original_tx, commitment_tx, precompile_address, input, commit_block, max_poll_block, contract expiry, resets state to AwaitingExecution, and clears execution/delivery results and proofs when canonical commitment context changes. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: This only matters under reorgs or canonical updates where the same origin hash is reprocessed with different commitment context.
statement: When adding a commitment for an origin transaction already present in the async pool, if commit_block, precompile address, or precompile input changed, the pool must replace the stored canonical context and clear previously submitted execution, delivery, and proof data tied to the old context.
suggested_falsification_tests: Insert an async pool entry with one commitment context and submitted results/proofs, then add the same origin hash with different commit_block/input/precompile; assert the stored context updates and all old result/proof fields are cleared.
summary: Duplicate origins with changed canonical context reset async pool state and clear stale proofs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert an async pool entry with one commitment context and submitted results/proofs, then add the same origin hash with different commit_block/input/precompile; assert the stored context updates and all old result/proof fields are cleared.
title: Async pool refreshes stale canonical context
